steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is the passage's statement that it would have been “impertinent to inquire too closely into the actual achievements his half-century of public service had produced”—a phrase that politely signals his accomplishments are essentially unclear.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly captures the tension: distinguished appearance but uncertain accomplishments.

The other three contradict the passage's central irony—by claiming capable service, inventing dishonor, or reframing uncertainty as hidden achievement.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
